title: Add edge case tests for empty/null PR body and diff
description:
## What

Add unit tests that cover edge cases where PR body is empty, null, or the diff is empty.

## Why

While the code handles these cases, we don't have explicit tests proving it. Better coverage prevents regressions.

## Test cases to add

### PR Description (tests/unit/analyzers/pr-description.test.ts)
- Empty body
- Body with only whitespace
- Very short body ("fix")
- Body with only emoji
- Body with only URLs

### PR Diff (tests/unit/analyzers/pr-diff.test.ts)
- Empty diff string
- Diff

raw_data.title: Add edge case tests for empty/null PR body and diff
raw_data.body:
## What

Add unit tests that cover edge cases where PR body is empty, null, or the diff is empty.

## Why

While the code handles these cases, we don't have explicit tests proving it. Better coverage prevents regressions.

## Test cases to add

### PR Description (tests/unit/analyzers/pr-description.test.ts)
- Empty body
- Body with only whitespace
- Very short body ("fix")
- Body with only emoji
- Body with only URLs

### PR Diff (tests/unit/analyzers/pr-diff.test.ts)
- Empty diff string
- Diff with only binary files
- Diff with only deleted files (no additions)
- Single file, single line change

### PR Commits (tests/unit/analyzers/pr-commits.test.ts)
- Empty commits array
- Single commit with empty message

## How to contribute

1. Fork the repo, create a branch
2. Add test cases to existing test files in `tests/unit/analyzers/`
3. Look at existing tests for patterns — they use Vitest (`describe`, `it`, `expect`)
4. Run `npm test` to verify
5. Open a PR

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At least 6 new edge case tests
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Tests are descriptive (good `it('should ...')` messages)

description:
## User Request

**Type:** bug
**Target:** Console Application
**Submitted by:** @Abhishek-Punhani
**Console Request ID:** 99f9bd2f-b1a9-47b8-a627-d476b89e97cf

## Description

## Description
The dashboard displays 6 unreachable clusters even when no clusters are running. These are "ghost" clusters—stale data persisted in `localStorage` that is not cleared when the application loses connection to the `kc-agent`.

## Root Cause Analysis
1.  **Persistence Layer**: The [web/src/hooks/mcp/shared.ts]

raw_data.title: 🐛  Ghost Clusters shown in Dashboard when Agent is Offline

labels.name: help wanted
labels.description: Denotes an issue that needs help from a contributor. Must meet "help wanted" guidelines.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

raw_data.body:
## User Request

**Type:** bug
**Target:** Console Application
**Submitted by:** @Abhishek-Punhani
**Console Request ID:** 99f9bd2f-b1a9-47b8-a627-d476b89e97cf

## Description

## Description
The dashboard displays 6 unreachable clusters even when no clusters are running. These are "ghost" clusters—stale data persisted in `localStorage` that is not cleared when the application loses connection to the `kc-agent`.

## Root Cause Analysis
1.  **Persistence Layer**: The [web/src/hooks/mcp/shared.ts](file:///home/manav/dev_ws/kubestellar/console/web/src/hooks/mcp/shared.ts) module saves cluster data to `localStorage` under the key `kubestellar-cluster-cache`.
2.  **Stale Cache**: When the app starts or refreshes, it initializes from this cache. If the `kc-agent` is offline or unreachable, the [fullFetchClusters](file:///home/manav/dev_ws/kubestellar/console/web/src/hooks/mcp/shared.ts#1236-1438) function fails to get a new list but **does not clear or invalidate** the existing cached list.
3.  **Deduplication Logic**: The [deduplicateClustersByServer](file:///home/manav/dev_ws/kubestellar/console/web/src/hooks/mcp/shared.ts#410-541) function merges multiple kubeconfig contexts pointing to the same server (common in OpenShift). The user likely has multiple contexts pointing to 6 unique servers.
4.  **Health Reporting**: Stale clusters that haven't been successfully health-checked recently are marked as `reachable: false` and `healthy: false`.
5.  **Dashboard Display**: The main dashboard's "Errors" stat block counts all `!healthy` clusters, resulting in the "6 Errors" display.

## Proposed Fix
-   **Cache Invalidation**: [fullFetchClusters](file:///home/manav/dev_ws/kubestellar/console/web/src/hooks/mcp/shared.ts#1236-1438) should clear the cluster cache if the agent is confirmed offline and no backend data is available.
-   **Stale Data Indicator**: Alternatively, the UI should clearly indicate that the cluster data is "Stale" or "Cached from [Date]" when the agent is unavailable.
-   **Dashboard Filtering**: The "Errors" block on the dashboard should potentially exclude unreachable clusters to avoid alarm when the agent itself is the one failing.

## Reproduction Steps
1.  Connect to an agent with 6+ clusters.
2.  Verify they appear on the dashboard.
3.  Stop the `kc-agent`.
4.  Refresh the dashboard.
5.  Observe that the 6 clusters still appear as "Errors" despite the agent being offline.

---
*This issue was automatically created from the KubeStellar Console.*

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

raw_data.body:
## User Request

**Type:** bug
**Target:** Console Application
**Submitted by:** @Arpit529Srivastava
**Console Request ID:** cc3e62a5-7e18-4c13-be4d-a4e327683b46

## Description

What happened:
Some frontend API calls use a fallback base URL of http://localhost:8080 when VITE_API_BASE_URL is not set. In real deployments (custom domain, ingress, reverse proxy), these calls can go to the wrong host and fail.

What I expected:
If no explicit API base URL is set, requests should default to same-origin relative URLs so deployed users can still use notifications, rewards, and feedback reliably.

Steps to reproduce:

Deploy console to any non-localhost domain.
Do not set VITE_API_BASE_URL.
Open features using notifications/rewards/feedback/nightly APIs.
Observe failed requests or requests targeting localhost.
Code context (file/line):

network.ts:37
useNotificationAPI.ts:6
useGitHubRewards.ts:50
FeatureRequestModal.tsx:142
FeatureRequestModal.tsx:160
FeatureRequestModal.tsx:193
NightlyE2EStatus.tsx:145
User impact:
Users see broken actions in production (test notifications, rewards refresh, feedback preview/login redirect), even though the app UI loads.

---
*This issue was automatically created from the KubeStellar Console.*

raw_data.title: 🌱 Add Unit Tests for Snooze Hooks (`Alerts`, `Cards`, `Missions`, `Recommendations`)

labels.name: help wanted
labels.description: Denotes an issue that needs help from a contributor. Must meet "help wanted" guidelines.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

raw_data.body:
## User Request

**Type:** bug
**Target:** Console Application
**Submitted by:** @Abhishek-Punhani
**Console Request ID:** ad954076-0d66-497c-b44e-aa27fdc30f9a

## Description

This issue tracks the addition of unit tests for the four hooks managing snooze/dismissal logic. These hooks share a **module-level singleton state** pattern (pub/sub), which allows state sharing across all instances but presents specific challenges for test isolation.

### Implementation Audit Notes:

*   **Singleton Pattern**: All four hooks use module-level variables (not React state) for the source of truth. Tests **MUST** demonstrate proper isolation by resetting this module state between test cases (e.g., using `vi.resetModules()`).
*   **`useSnoozedAlerts`**:
    - Persists to `localStorage`.
    - Auto-cleans expired entries on load.
    - Uses a polling interval for auto-removal.
*   **`useSnoozedRecommendations`**:
    - **[TYPO FOUND]**: The function is named `unsnooozeRecommendation` (extra 'o'). The test suite should account for this or document it for a future fix.
*   **`useSnoozedMissions`**:
    - Combines `snoozed` (expiring) and `dismissed` (permanent) state.

## Test Scenarios

### Common Logic
- [ ] **Cross-instance Reactivity**: Verifies that calling a mutation on one hook instance updates all others.
- [ ] **Isolation**: Verifies that state from one test does not leak into the next (singleton reset).

### `useSnoozedAlerts`
- [ ] **Initial Sync**: Loads and filters expired entries from `localStorage` on mount.
- [ ] **Expiry Polling**: Verifies auto-removal of expired snoozes using `vi.useFakeTimers()`.
- [ ] **Time Remaining**: Verifies `getSnoozeRemaining` and `formatSnoozeRemaining` accuracy.

### `useSnoozedCards` & `useSnoozedRecommendations`
- [ ] **In-Memory Logic**: Verifies active vs expired swap filtering.
- [ ] **Typo Verification**: Verifies `unsnooozeRecommendation` behaves as expected despite the naming.
- [ ] **Idempotency**: Verifies that snoozing the same item twice doesn't create duplicate entries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ≥ 8 test cases per hook.
- [ ] Timer-based expiry and module singleton isolation verified.
- [ ] `localStorage` side effects verified (for `Alerts` and `Missions`).
- [ ] All tests pass `npm test`.

---
*This issue was automatically created from the KubeStellar Console.*

title: Add file extension mappings for missing languages
description:
## What

Add more file extension to language mappings to `EXTENSION_MAP` in `src/utils/language-detect.ts`.

## Why

The language detection is used by the diff parser to classify files. We currently support ~35 extensions but are missing several common ones.

## Missing extensions

- `.m` → Objective-C
- `.mm` → Objective-C++
- `.pl` / `.pm` → Perl
- `.f90` / `.f95` → Fortran
- `.jl` → Julia
- `.clj` / `.cljs` → Clojure
- `.fs` / `.fsx` → F#
- `.hs` → Haskell
- `.ml` / `.mli` → OCaml
- `.cr` → C

raw_data.title: Add file extension mappings for missing languages
raw_data.body:
## What

Add more file extension to language mappings to `EXTENSION_MAP` in `src/utils/language-detect.ts`.

## Why

The language detection is used by the diff parser to classify files. We currently support ~35 extensions but are missing several common ones.

## Missing extensions

- `.m` → Objective-C
- `.mm` → Objective-C++
- `.pl` / `.pm` → Perl
- `.f90` / `.f95` → Fortran
- `.jl` → Julia
- `.clj` / `.cljs` → Clojure
- `.fs` / `.fsx` → F#
- `.hs` → Haskell
- `.ml` / `.mli` → OCaml
- `.cr` → Crystal
- `.tf` / `.hcl` → HCL (Terraform)
- `.sol` → Solidity

## How to contribute

1. Fork the repo, create a branch
2. Add mappings to `EXTENSION_MAP` in `src/utils/language-detect.ts`
3. Use lowercase language names (e.g., `'haskell'`, `'perl'`)
4. Run `npm test` to verify nothing breaks
5. Open a PR

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At least 8 new extensions added
- [ ] Language names follow existing convention (lowercase)
- [ ] `npm test` passes

title: Add more AI fluff word patterns to detection
description:
## What

Add more fluff words/phrases to the `FLUFF_WORDS` array in `src/utils/patterns.ts`.

## Why

LLMs overuse certain words and phrases that humans rarely write. Our current list has 25 patterns, but there are more common ones we're missing.

## Suggested additions

Research and add patterns for words like:
- `groundbreaking`
- `revolutionize` / `revolutionary`
- `game-changing` / `game changer`
- `transformative`
- `innovative` / `innovation`
- `meticulous(ly)`
- `intricate`
- `nuanced`
-

## What

Add more fluff words/phrases to the `FLUFF_WORDS` array in `src/utils/patterns.ts`.

## Why

LLMs overuse certain words and phrases that humans rarely write. Our current list has 25 patterns, but there are more common ones we're missing.

## Suggested additions

Research and add patterns for words like:
- `groundbreaking`
- `revolutionize` / `revolutionary`
- `game-changing` / `game changer`
- `transformative`
- `innovative` / `innovation`
- `meticulous(ly)`
- `intricate`
- `nuanced`
- `tapestry`
- `realm`
- `landscape` (in non-geographic context)
- `underscore` (verb usage)

## How to contribute

1. Fork the repo, create a branch
2. Add new regex patterns to `FLUFF_WORDS` in `src/utils/patterns.ts`
3. Each pattern should use `\b` word boundaries and the `i` flag
4. Add a test case in `tests/unit/analyzers/pr-description.test.ts`
5. Run `npm test` to verify
6. Open a PR

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At least 5 new patterns added
- [ ] Each pattern uses word boundaries
- [ ] No false positives on common English
- [ ] Tests pass

## 🎌 Add Japanese Proverb (ことわざ)

**Category:** Community Contribution - Proverb  
**Difficulty:** Easy (good first issue!)  
**Estimated Time:** <1 min

---

### 🎯 Your Task

Add this traditional Japanese proverb to help learners understand Japanese wisdom!

### The Proverb

| Japanese | Reading | English |
|----------|---------|---------|
| **井戸端会議** | Idobata kaigi | Well-side conference |

> 💡 **Meaning:** Neighborhood gossip

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/japanese-proverbs.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japanese-proverbs.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following proverb object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "japanese": "井戸端会議",
  "romaji": "Idobata kaigi",
  "english": "Well-side conference",
  "meaning": "Neighborhood gossip"
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add new japanese proverb`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

---

labels.name: help wanted
labels.description: Denotes an issue that needs help from a contributor. Must meet "help wanted" guidelines.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

raw_data.body:
## User Request

**Type:** bug
**Target:** Console Application
**Submitted by:** @Arpit529Srivastava
**Console Request ID:** ae2830a9-bc0e-473a-b59a-c88065e2cdfa

## Description

What happened:
When deployment env vars are missing, feedback and rewards logic defaults to kubestellar repo/org targets. Forks or enterprise deployments can unintentionally route user actions to upstream repos.

What I expected:
Repo owner/name and rewards org query should be explicitly configured or derived from deployment profile, not hardcoded upstream defaults.

Steps to reproduce:

Deploy a forked or white-label version.
Leave FEEDBACK_REPO_OWNER, FEEDBACK_REPO_NAME, and REWARDS_GITHUB_ORGS unset.
Use feedback submission or rewards features.
Observe targeting of kubestellar repos/orgs.
Code context (file/line):
server.go:1105
server.go:1106
server.go:1108

User impact:
User feedback may be sent to the wrong repository, and rewards data may not represent the deployment’s own project activity.

---
*This issue was automatically created from the KubeStellar Console.*

## Enhancement: Add Preset Configuration for Easier Onboarding

### Summary
Introduce an optional `preset` field in the API options to provide sensible default configurations. This reduces the need for manual tuning of parameters like `fps`, `pattern`, and `distortion`, improving the onboarding experience.

---

### Problem
Currently, users must manually configure parameters such as:
- `fps`
- `pattern`
- `distortion`

This increases setup time and creates unnecessary friction, especially for new users.

---

### Proposed Solution
Add an optional `preset` field to the API options:

```ts
type Preset = "low" | "medium" | "high";

title: Improve emoji detection to handle compound emoji (ZWJ sequences)
description:
## What

The current emoji counter in `src/utils/text-utils.ts` may over-count compound emoji (ZWJ sequences like family emoji count as multiple matches instead of one).

## Why

ZWJ (Zero Width Joiner) emoji like profession emoji and flag sequences are composed of multiple code points joined by U+200D. Our regex counts each component separately, inflating the count.

## What to do

1. Research how ZWJ emoji work
2. Update `countEmojis()` in `src/utils/text-utils.ts`
3. Options:
   - Use `Intl.S

raw_data.title: Improve emoji detection to handle compound emoji (ZWJ sequences)
raw_data.body:
## What

The current emoji counter in `src/utils/text-utils.ts` may over-count compound emoji (ZWJ sequences like family emoji count as multiple matches instead of one).

## Why

ZWJ (Zero Width Joiner) emoji like profession emoji and flag sequences are composed of multiple code points joined by U+200D. Our regex counts each component separately, inflating the count.

## What to do

1. Research how ZWJ emoji work
2. Update `countEmojis()` in `src/utils/text-utils.ts`
3. Options:
   - Use `Intl.Segmenter` (available in Node 20, our target)
   - Or adjust the regex to consume ZWJ sequences as single matches
4. Add test cases with ZWJ emoji

## Test cases

- Single ZWJ emoji should count as 1
- Skin tone modifier emoji should count as 1
- Flag emoji should count as 1
- Mixed regular + ZWJ emoji should count correctly

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ZWJ sequences count as single emoji
- [ ] Skin tone modifiers don't inflate count
- [ ] Existing tests still pass
- [ ] `npm test` passes

title: Add more formal/bureaucratic phrases for issue detection
description:
## What

Expand the `FORMAL_PHRASES` array in `src/utils/patterns.ts` with more patterns typical of AI-generated issue reports.

## Why

AI-generated bug reports often use an overly formal, corporate tone. Our current list has 20 patterns but misses several common ones.

## Suggested additions

- "I am writing to inform you"
- "I hope this message finds you well"
- "Please do not hesitate to"
- "Your prompt attention to this matter"
- "at your earliest convenience"
- "I would be grateful if"
- "

raw_data.title: Add more formal/bureaucratic phrases for issue detection
raw_data.body:
## What

Expand the `FORMAL_PHRASES` array in `src/utils/patterns.ts` with more patterns typical of AI-generated issue reports.

## Why

AI-generated bug reports often use an overly formal, corporate tone. Our current list has 20 patterns but misses several common ones.

## Suggested additions

- "I am writing to inform you"
- "I hope this message finds you well"
- "Please do not hesitate to"
- "Your prompt attention to this matter"
- "at your earliest convenience"
- "I would be grateful if"
- "Respectfully submitted"
- "Thank you for your consideration"
- "herein" / "aforementioned" / "henceforth"
- "the above-mentioned"

## How to contribute

1. Fork the repo, create a branch
2. Add new regex patterns to `FORMAL_PHRASES` in `src/utils/patterns.ts`
3. Add test cases in `tests/unit/analyzers/issue-content.test.ts`
4. Run `npm test`
5. Open a PR

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At least 5 new patterns added
- [ ] Each uses word boundaries where appropriate
- [ ] Tests verify detection works
- [ ] No false positives on reasonable technical writing

## Summary
Add deterministic rendering tests and visual snapshots to catch subtle output regressions.

## Problem
Unit tests may pass while visual/temporal quality regresses.

## Proposed Solution
- [ ] Add deterministic seed mode for temporal/noise engines
- [ ] Capture key frame snapshots in tests

- [ ] Tests are reproducible across runs

## Labels
`quality` `testing` `ci` `priority:high`

Manual option tuning is inconsistent across apps and hard for new users.

- [ ] Add expert knobs: layer count, temporal jitter, noise amplitude, distortion intensity
- [ ] Allow expert values to override profile defaults
- [ ] Expose active profile metadata for UI/logging

raw_data.title: Security Strength Profiles + Expert Controls
raw_data.body:
## Summary
Introduce built-in security profiles and advanced controls for predictable protection tuning.

## Problem
Manual option tuning is inconsistent across apps and hard for new users.

## Proposed Solution
- [ ] Add profiles: `baseline`, `balanced`, `hardened`
- [ ] Add expert knobs: layer count, temporal jitter, noise amplitude, distortion intensity
- [ ] Allow expert values to override profile defaults
- [ ] Expose active profile metadata for UI/logging

## Scope
- [ ] Profile schema + validation
- [ ] Option parsing (backward compatible)
- [ ] Mapping profile -> runtime settings
- [ ] Demo profile selector + advanced panel
- [ ] Tests for profile determinism and overrides

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Profiles produce distinct, repeatable output
- [ ] Overrides apply predictably
- [ ] Invalid combos return clear errors
- [ ] README includes migration + examples

## Labels
`enhancement` `feature` `api` `priority:high`

title: Adaptive Security Mode (Auto-Tune by Device Performance)

## Summary
Add an adaptive mode that dynamically tunes rendering behavior based on real-time performance and page visibility.

## Problem
Static settings can overuse CPU/GPU on low-end devices and underuse high-end devices.

## Proposed Solution
- [ ] Add an `adaptive` option in render config.
- [ ] Track rolling frame-time and dropped-frame ratio.
- [ ] Auto-adjust FPS target, pattern complexity, and distortion strength.
- [ ] Reduce workload when tab is hidden; recover smoothly on focus.

raw_data.title: Adaptive Security Mode (Auto-Tune by Device Performance)
raw_data.body:
## Summary
Add an adaptive mode that dynamically tunes rendering behavior based on real-time performance and page visibility.

## Problem
Static settings can overuse CPU/GPU on low-end devices and underuse high-end devices.

## Proposed Solution
- [ ] Add an `adaptive` option in render config.
- [ ] Track rolling frame-time and dropped-frame ratio.
- [ ] Auto-adjust FPS target, pattern complexity, and distortion strength.
- [ ] Reduce workload when tab is hidden; recover smoothly on focus.

## Scope
- [ ] Runtime performance monitor
- [ ] Adaptive policy engine with thresholds
- [ ] Public API option docs
- [ ] Demo controls + adaptive status indicator
- [ ] Tests for adaptive transitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stable output under normal load
- [ ] Graceful downshift on weak devices
- [ ] Quality uplift on strong devices without stutter
- [ ] No loop leaks after repeated hide/show
- [ ] Backward compatible when `adaptive` is disabled

## Labels
`enhancement` `feature` `performance` `priority:high`
